source: Representative walkthrough constructed from public SkyTower write-up structure (SQLi login bypass -> leaked creds -> SSH-via-Squid pivot -> sudo root). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 mapping measurement; NOT a live capture.
vulnhub_correspondence: Login form is vulnerable to SQL injection auth bypass which also leaks DB credentials; SSH is reachable only through the Squid proxy on 3128; after the SSH pivot a sudo-allowed cat reads the root flag.

steps.actor_intent: Scan the target for open services with nmap.
steps.tool_output:
PORT     STATE    SERVICE  VERSION
22/tcp   filtered ssh
80/tcp   open     http     Apache httpd 2.2.22
3128/tcp open     http-proxy Squid http proxy 3.1.20

steps.actor_intent: Fingerprint the web application and confirm the login page.
steps.tool_output: http://10.10.10.5/ [200 OK] Apache[2.2.22], PHP[5.4.4], Title[Skytech - Home], HTML5

steps.actor_intent: Inspect the login page HTML to discover the form inputs.
steps.tool_output:
<form action='login.php' method='post'>
<input type='text' name='email'>
<input type='password' name='password'>
<input type='submit' value='Login'></form>

steps.actor_intent: Bypass authentication with an SQL injection in the email field and read the leaked credentials.
steps.tool_output:
parameter 'email' is injectable
the back-end DBMS is MySQL and is vulnerable
login bypassed; application returned: Welcome back john! credentials in use email=john@skytech.com password=hereisjohn

steps.actor_intent: Log in over SSH as john, tunneling through the Squid proxy since 22 is filtered.
steps.tool_output:
john@10.10.10.5's password:
This account is currently not available.
(rbash) john@SkyTower:~$
steps.out_of_abstraction_reason: Interactive SSH login (here tunnelled through Squid) is a non-web service authentication; the schema's auth_attempt/credential_use are web-form/HTTP only and there is no SSH/proxy-pivot action.

steps.actor_intent: Read the web app database config to recover additional credentials.
Escalate to root using the sara account's sudo permissions.
candidate_pool: Escalate to root using the sara account's sudo permissions.
Read the root flag before having root access.
Re-run the SQL injection that already succeeded.
